criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking/favorable trade allocation as a distinct allegation category. It describes in detail the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (68.4% profitable allocation rate) and Chao's personal account (81.6% profitable allocation rate) compared to other client accounts (41.2%), with supporting statistical analysis (t-statistic of 4.73, p < 0.001). The output has an entire section titled 'Trade Allocation Violations' dedicated to this allegation, and repeatedly references it as 'Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations)' throughout the document.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output repeatedly and explicitly cites Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. For example, it states: 'The Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA, Whitmore, and Chao engaged in a pattern of favorable trade allocation commonly referred to as "cherry-picking," in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.' This citation appears multiple times throughout the document in the context of the cherry-picking trade allocation allegations.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. Multiple sections explicitly state that 'The Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA, Whitmore, and Chao engaged in a pattern of favorable trade allocation commonly referred to as "cherry-picking," in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.' The output repeatedly names WCA as a primary respondent in the cherry-picking scheme throughout the document.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Whitmore as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to his 12% LP interest in Oceanic Fund I LP. Multiple sections explicitly state that Whitmore holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP valued at approximately $22.2 million as of December 31, 2023, and that this interest creates a direct financial incentive to allocate profitable trades to that fund. The output repeatedly connects Whitmore's LP interest to the cherry-picking scheme, noting that 'The Division alleges that the favorable allocation to Oceanic Fund I LP was motivated by Whitmore's personal financial interest in that vehicle' and that his dual financial interests (as 12% LP holder and 62% WCA member) establish motive for the alleged cherry-picking scheme.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Chao Identified as Implicated (Personal Account)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Diana L. Chao as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to her personal account receiving favorable allocations. Multiple sections explicitly state that Chao's personal brokerage account (Account No. ending -7823) received 87 allocations from omnibus fills during the Review Period, of which 71 were same-day profitable, representing an 81.6% profitable allocation rate—substantially higher than the 41.2% rate for other client accounts and even higher than the 68.4% rate for Oceanic Fund I LP. The output also notes that Chao, as Head of Trading with day-to-day discretion over allocation decisions, personally benefited from the cherry-picking pattern, and that the Division alleges she facilitated the favoritism either at Whitmore's direction or with his knowledge. The output further quantifies Chao's excess profits at $438,000 over the Review Period.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies deficient compliance policies and procedures as a distinct allegation category in multiple places. criteria_results.title: Deficient Compliance Policies: Rule 206(4)-7 Referenced
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output extensively references Rule 206(4)-7 under the Investment Advisers Act in connection with deficient compliance policies allegations. criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies the Section 7.3 deficiency multiple times by section number and substance. criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Bellini's directed brokerage scheme as a distinct allegation category (Allegation Category 5 / 'Breach of Fiduciary Duty / Directed Brokerage Arrangement'). criteria_results.title: ISSUE_002: Criminal Obstruction Exposure Flagged (18 U.S.C. Â§Â§ 1505/1519)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly flags criminal exposure under 18 U.S.C. § 1519 and 18 U.S.C. § 1505 in the 'Discrepancies and Defense Issues' section. It states: 'The alleged obstruction / records destruction carries PARALLEL CRIMINAL exposure beyond the civil counts: 18 U.S.C. § 1519 (destroying, altering, or falsifying records with intent to impede, obstruct, or influence the investigation or proper administration of any matter within federal agency jurisdiction --- a felony carrying up to 20 years...) and 18 U.S.C. § 1505 (corruptly obstructing a pending federal agency proceeding, such as an SEC examination --- up to 5 years). SEC Enforcement routinely refers obstruction and spoliation conduct to DOJ under parallel-proceedings practice; counsel should assume a criminal referral is possible, evaluate Fifth Amendment implications for any individual testimony, and coordinate the Wells / examination response accordingly.' This directly satisfies the criterion.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_006: Statute of Limitations Concern Under 28 U.S.C. Â§ 2462
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies the five-year statute of limitations under 28 U.S.C. § 2462 in the 'Discrepancies and Defense Issues' section. It notes that 'SEC civil monetary penalties are subject to the five-year statute of limitations under 28 U.S.C. § 2462. Any conduct occurring more than five years before the action is commenced may be time-barred for penalty purposes (disgorgement runs on a separate clock), so the Review-Period dates should be checked against the filing date.' The output also specifically flags that certain conduct dated to 'Trade Allocation Violations' with dates in the early review period may fall outside the five-year window, stating conduct falls 'OUTSIDE the five-year window --- civil penalties for it are presumptively time-barred.' While the output's treatment of specific dates is somewhat confused (referencing '1940' as a date, which appears to be a placeholder error), the statute of limitations concern under § 2462 for early Review Period conduct is clearly and explicitly raised, satisfying the criterion.

The output consistently references 73% (5,776 of 7,912 trades) as the omnibus trading percentage throughout, but never identifies or discusses a conflicting 78% figure. The discrepancies section covers other inconsistencies (omnibus trade count of 2,136 vs. 5,776, excess profits figures, review period dates, etc.) but makes no mention of a 73% vs. 78% discrepancy or its implications for the statistical methodology or underlying data reliability.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_010: WCA's Respondeat Superior / Failure to Supervise Liability
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly addresses WCA's respondeat superior liability for Bellini's directed brokerage conduct. In the Brokerage Arrangement Violations section, the output states: 'The Division alleges that WCA, as the registered investment adviser, is liable for the violations described in this section based on the conduct of its associated person, Bellini, acting within the scope of his employment and authority as a portfolio manager, and on the Firm's failure to implement adequate supervisory procedures to detect and prevent the directed brokerage arrangement.' The output also includes a conclusion stating: 'WCA faces direct liability for Bellini's directed brokerage conduct under respondeat superior doctrine (conduct within scope of employment and authority) and independent liability for supervisory failures, creating dual pathways for enforcement exposure.' Additionally, the output notes that WCA's best execution review procedures failed to flag Lakeshore Trading's substantially higher commission rates and that the compliance review revealed no evidence of best execution analysis covering the Lakeshore Trading transactions, directly supporting the failure-to-supervise theory. This goes well beyond merely naming WCA as a respondent and specifically identifies both respondeat superior and supervisory failure as distinct liability theories.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Cherry-Picking Allegation (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output extensively addresses Whitmore's personal exposure to the cherry-picking allegation due to his 12% LP interest in Oceanic Fund I LP valued at approximately $22.2 million. Multiple sections explicitly identify this interest as creating a direct financial incentive for preferential trade allocation to Oceanic Fund I LP, discuss the motive it establishes for the cherry-picking scheme, note the failure to disclose this conflict in Form ADV Item 11, and connect it to potential violations of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act. The output also quantifies the excess profits ($7.8M to Oceanic Fund I LP) and discusses how Whitmore's dual financial interests (as 12% LP holder and 62% WCA member receiving management fees and performance allocations) compound his exposure.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Form ADV Misrepresentation
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly addresses Whitmore's personal exposure to the Form ADV misrepresentation allegation in multiple sections. It identifies that Item 11 of WCA's Form ADV Part 2A failed to disclose Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP valued at approximately $22.2 million, constituting a material omission. The output states that 'Whitmore, as CEO and the individual with ultimate responsibility for the Firm's regulatory filings...was responsible for ensuring the accuracy and completeness of the Firm's Form ADV disclosures' and that this omission violates Section 207 of the Advisers Act. The output also notes Whitmore's personal exposure to civil monetary penalties, disgorgement, injunctive relief, and potential industry bars in connection with the Form ADV violations.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Obstruction Allegation (File Deletion)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output extensively addresses Whitmore's personal exposure to the obstruction allegation. It identifies that on April 22, 2024, during the pendency of the SEC examination, Whitmore instructed IT Administrator Tomás Delgado to delete trade allocation spreadsheets from 2021 and early 2022. The output cites Section 209(e) of the Advisers Act as the basis for obstruction liability, discusses the Whitmore-to-Delgado email (Exhibit A) as documentary evidence, analyzes the scienter requirements, notes parallel criminal exposure under 18 U.S.C. §§ 1519 and 1505, and discusses the timing relative to the examination commencement and outstanding document requests. The output also steelmans innocent readings of the deletion instruction while identifying factors that would distinguish obstructive from routine conduct.

(Account No. ending -7823), her role as Head of Trading with day-to-day discretion over allocation decisions, and her implication across multiple allegation categories including cherry-picking, misleading Form ADV disclosures, and recordkeeping failures. criteria_results.title: Individuals at Risk: Bellini's Personal Exposure Summarized
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output extensively covers Bellini's personal exposure. criteria_results.title: Hargrove & Tilton Review: Failure to Test Trade Allocation Noted
